evals.prompt:
Create a new Slidev deck for my EuroPython 2026 talk. Here's the proposal:

## Title
A reliable development/release workflow for open source Python libraries

## Track
Tooling, Packaging, Developer Productivity

## Abstract
This talk provides a complete blueprint for architecting a development and release workflow specifically for open source Python libraries published to PyPI. We move beyond basic test-build-deploy workflows to address more topics necessary for developing and maintaining trusted libraries: solid test-build strategies, changelog management, handling untrusted contributions, managing feature branch preview builds, automated semantic versioning, documentation generation, ensuring package quality, and securing the release process and final distribution.

## Outline
- Introduction (2 mins)
- Solid test and build strategy (5 mins)
- Change management (5 mins)
- Security in open source (5 mins)
- Documentation (5 mins)
- Developer expertise (3 mins)
- Q&A (5 mins)

## Expected audience expertise
Intermediate
evals.expected_output: A new deck directory under decks/ with package.json and slides.md following the author's established patterns

evals.prompt:
Create a deck for my PyCon US 2025 talk based on this proposal:

# Title
In-browser AI apps with Streamlit, Gradio, and Transformers

# Description
You can build serverless web-based AI apps that run entirely in the browser using only Python. The Python ecosystem already has the building blocks: UI frameworks like Streamlit and Gradio, and AI model libraries like Transformers, and they have in-browser versions! By embedding Python code with these libraries directly into an HTML file and serving it from a static web server, you can host an AI application featuring a rich UI and powerful AI models that users can interact with in their browser.

# Outline
## Intro (5mins)
* What are AI apps
* General intro for the Python libraries for AI app development
* What are in-browser AI apps
* The benefits of in-browser AI apps

## Technical Overview (5mins)
* Python on frontend: Pyodide
* Pyodide-versions of the Python libraries, Stlite, Gradio-Lite, and Transformers.js

## How-to (5mins)
* Getting-started tutorial of the libraries

## Examples (5mins)
* Examples of in-browser AI apps that you can build with the libraries

## Deployment/Delivery (5mins)
* How to deploy the in-browser AI apps

## Discussions (5mins)
* Pros and cons of in-browser AI apps and the Python-based development approach
* Applicable use cases

# Category
Machine Learning: Frameworks

# Audience
Some experience
evals.expected_output: A new deck with code examples, architecture diagrams (using anipres/fancy-arrow), and the author's typical animation style

evals.prompt: Add a new slide to decks/202602-oss-give-and-take after the 'Programming is over?' slide. It should show a comparison between traditional software development and AI-assisted development, with bullet points appearing one by one.
evals.expected_output: A modified slides.md with a new slide inserted at the right position, using v-clicks for progressive reveal
